title: True Grit: Wilderland Liberation
blurb: Oregon Trail–style comic frames. An original thrall-to-liberator arc through the Wilderland — warrants, orcs, and Candlemire’s collar yards. A scarred freehold marshal and a stubborn freeman who will not quit. Inspired by liberation-Western grit; no film trademark character names.

encounterCadence.note: Engine-side: roll next random ambush after 10–20 frames advance; prefer chapter enemyThemes.

chapters.title: Chain-Road Dawn
chapters.tagline: A numbered thrall is bought off a road of cages — and asked to kill for paper freedom.

chapters.title: Dust and Debt
chapters.tagline: Warrants pay in silver and scars while Quill teaches steel that answers to no collar.

chapters.title: The Wanted Mark
chapters.tagline: A carved brand leads east; friends and hunters argue over who owns the man who escaped.

chapters.title: River of Brands
chapters.tagline: Barges haul branded cargo across a mud-brown river the freeholds pretend not to see.

chapters.title: Candlemire Gates
chapters.tagline: Lord Cade's border keeps smile for coin and iron for mercy; Lyra is somewhere past the smoke.

chapters.title: House of Collars
chapters.tagline: Guest rooms overlook yards of rings and numbers; friendship here is whispered at risk of skin.

chapters.title: Blood Mandolin
chapters.tagline: Candlemire's sport pits fighters under lanterns while a mandolin keeps time with screams.

chapters.title: Liberation March
chapters.tagline: Cages open. Brands break. The field that grew cane learns a different crop: uprising.

chapters.title: Free Horizon
chapters.tagline: Roads west remember every foot that left a collar behind — and ask what freedom costs next.

nodes.title: Cage Line
nodes.body: Mist sits on the Chain-Road like damp wool soaked in livestock math. Iron cages rattle on ox-wains while overseers count numbers instead of names — and spit when the count comes up short. You are Nine-Mark: thrall, fighter, and cargo that punches back when the whip gets lazy.

nodes.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Collar Echo
nodes.body: Marshal Halbrecht Quill lights a match off a warrant nail, drinks, and looks almost human. Freedom still feels like hunger wearing better boots. West is a rumor; east is a debt with a smile.

nodes.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Warrant Weather
nodes.body: Camp smoke writes temporary law across the pines. Tonight’s camp will argue theology with mosquitoes and memory. Quill packs the silence like powder — dry, useful, dangerous.

nodes.title: Road Vermin Tax
nodes.body: Three road vermin block a ford with a rope and a grin. Their leader — missing two fingers and all shame — offers “protection” for coin, a story, or a bruise. Quill’s hand rests near his bolt like punctuation.

choices.label: Pay the damn rope
choices.approach: Buy the ford; save steel for Vern.

outcome.text: They salute with stolen manners. Quill mutters, “Inflation.”

choices.label: Scare them honest
choices.approach: Show the freemark and the crossbow’s opinion.

outcome.text: Rope drops. One kid whispers, “Nine-Mark walks.” Word travels usefully.

choices.label: Trade a Chain-Road story
choices.approach: Give them a tale sharp enough to sell twice.

success.text: They point you to a dry cache under the ford stones — amateurs with useful greed.

fail.text: They laugh wrong. Quill pays anyway and files the insult.

nodes.title: Quill's Ugly Joke
nodes.body: At dusk Quill asks whether you want to be a freeman who hunts, or a hunter who happens to be free — then drinks like the answer might offend him. The difference, he says, is who you apologize to when the smoke clears. “And don’t you damn quit either way.”

choices.label: Freeman who hunts
choices.approach: Keep the moral order: freedom first, warrants second.

outcome.text: “Then we hunt like guests,” Quill says. “Violent guests. Stubborn ones.”

choices.label: Hunter who is free
choices.approach: Admit the work shapes the man.

outcome.text: Quill almost smiles with the scarred eye. “Honest. Ugly. Useful.”

choices.label: Neither — Lyra’s man
choices.approach: Refuse Quill’s categories; keep the vow central.

outcome.text: “Categories are cages,” Quill allows. “Fine. Be a vow with a sword that won’t quit.”

nodes.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Ash Aside
nodes.body: Steel rides light; the flask in Quill’s coat does not. You catch yourself walking like cargo that learned a verb. Continue — soft roads make soft collars again.

nodes.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Quill's Aside
nodes.body: Lyra’s name sits behind your teeth like a loaded prayer. Quill drawls, “Paper freedom first. Real freedom files later. Don’t quit in between.” Another frame turns like a dusty page with teeth.

nodes.title: The Binder Arrives
nodes.body: A lean marshal in travel-stained grey tips a ruined hat to the overseer like a man haggling over mule feed and mildly disappointing weather. Halbrecht Quill — freehold marshal, warrant binder, one scarred eye that never quite closes on a lie — pays coin for your papers without looking away. “Pretty muscle,” he drawls, whiskey still on the breath. “Ugly price. I’ll take the stubborn one.”

nodes.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Cane Wind
nodes.body: Lyra’s name sits behind your teeth like a loaded prayer. Freedom still feels like hunger wearing better boots. Steel rests light; resolve does not.

nodes.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Dust Mile
nodes.body: Quill buys your freedom like a man purchasing a complicated knife — and a headache. The next mile pretends to be empty and fails the audition. Night takes attendance; you answer as a freeman.

nodes.title: Quill Names the Work
nodes.body: “I buy thralls who can ruin the men who sold them,” Quill says, not unkind, flask catching firelight. “If that offends your poetry, walk. If it fits your teeth, load — and if you quit mid-road, I’ll leave you for the crows with a receipt.” Ash loads.

nodes.title: Vern's Joke
nodes.body: A traveler repeats Vern’s favorite joke about unpaid tribute and burned kitchens. Nobody laughs. Quill tips the man a copper for the geography hidden in the cruelty, then mutters that hell of a comedian still makes hell of a warrant.

nodes.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Old Hunger
nodes.body: Lyra’s name sits behind your teeth like a loaded prayer. Road vermin sell water and buy stories about numbered men who would not quit. The cane smell thickens like a dare.

nodes.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Freemark Thoughts
nodes.body: Ash tastes copper on the wind and does not flinch. Quill says, “Pretty country. Ugly ownership. Pass the damn bottle.” Quill tips his ruined hat at unfinished justice and takes a pull.

nodes.title: Paper Freedom
nodes.body: Quill slides a stamped sheet across the wagon board and uncorks a dented flask like punctuation. Freedom on parchment, conditional as a hangman’s courtesy: hunt the guilty he names, keep steel sharp, ask no soft questions. “Say yes,” he offers, almost kindly, “or climb back into inventory. I don’t do poetry, and I don’t ride with quitters.”

choices.label: Take the warrant path
choices.approach: Accept conditional freedom; work for Quill.

outcome.text: Quill smiles without warmth. “Good. The road hates empty hands, full sermons, and soft guts.”

choices.label: Bargain for a name first
choices.approach: Ask whose name this work is really for.

success.text: He softens half a degree and takes a pull. “Lyra. Sold east years ago. Candlemire keeps pretty ledgers and uglier guests. You don’t quit easy — fine. Neither does that name.”

fail.text: Quill’s scarred eye narrows. “Earn the damn right to ask.” He still pushes the papers toward you.

choices.label: Test the man's honesty
choices.approach: Watch for a twitch that says he buys thralls for worse work.

success.text: No twitch — only tired calculation and a flask that never quite empties. Dangerous, yes. Aligned, for now. He notices you looking and tips his hat at the insult.

fail.text: You misread courtesy for cruelty. Quill shrugs. “Everyone does, once.” He hands you a short sword and a look that says don’t quit on the first mile.

nodes.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Ledger Weather
nodes.body: Quill hums a tune that never finishes the same way twice. A joke dies mid-laugh when Cade’s check appears on a sleeve. The next mile does not apologize.

nodes.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Steel Practice
nodes.body: Somebody buried a whip and marked the dirt with hope. Ash keeps counsel; Quill keeps a ledger of silences and a flask of worse truths. Ash sets the pace like a man owed a name and too stubborn to renegotiate.

nodes.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Night Watch
nodes.body: A child’s chalk freemark on a fence post has already been scrubbed. Quill drawls, “Paper freedom first. Real freedom files later. Don’t quit in between.” Another frame turns like a dusty page with teeth.

nodes.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Iron Humor
nodes.body: Ash tastes copper on the wind and does not flinch. Tracks braid, unbraid, and lie with professional cheer. Lyra’s sale date ticks quieter than a clock and louder than fear.

nodes.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Collar Echo
nodes.body: A hanged scarecrow wears Cade’s check like a joke that landed. Quill says, “Pretty country. Ugly ownership. Pass the damn bottle.” West is a rumor; east is a debt with a smile.

nodes.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Warrant Weather
nodes.body: Wagon ruts braid into a grammar only hunters read fluently. The road offers three bad choices and one worse delay. Dust settles on everything except the vow.

nodes.title: Collar Off
nodes.body: The overseer pries the collar with a hooked key that smells of rust, old sweat, and other people’s surrender. Skin burns where iron sat for seasons. Quill tosses the ring into roadside mud like trash that once owned a man — then grinds it under his heel, just to be thorough.

nodes.title: First Mile Free
nodes.body: Boots that never chose a direction now choose west, then south, at Quill’s nudge. Crows watch the cages dwindle and look personally offended. Freedom feels like hunger with better manners and a loaded crossbow for a chaperone.

nodes.body: Chain-Road mist sits on the world like damp wool and worse math. Ash refuses to romanticize the road; the road returns the favor. Night takes attendance; you answer as a freeman.

nodes.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Freemark Thoughts
nodes.body: Heat makes the freemark itch like unfinished business. Somewhere east, ink still invents people into inventory. The next mile does not apologize.

nodes.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Ledger Weather
nodes.body: Overseer laughter carries farther than any hymn on this road. Dust becomes a third companion with poor hygiene and worse jokes. Quill files Lowhedge under “paid in blood or nearly” and takes a pull.

nodes.body: Bootleather remembers every mile better than maps ever will. Quill’s tea tastes like boiled warrants, patience, and whiskey that lost an argument. Somewhere east, a ledger page waits to be rewritten.

nodes.title: Orc Chainers
nodes.body: Three chain-orcs peel from the pine edge, still smelling of the cages they sold this morning. They want their property returned with interest. Quill calmly loads a crossbow and drawls, “Show them the paperwork is yours — and that the warranty expired.”

choices.label: Stand and fight
choices.approach: Use hotbar steel; clocks stay off — take your time.

outcome.text: Chain-Orc Reclaimer falls. The road tastes like iron and relief.

choices.label: Outwit the terrain
choices.approach: Use cover, noise, and Quill’s nasty little tricks.

success.text: The ambush becomes your board. They break first.

fail.text: Clever turns loud. You bleed for the lesson and win anyway.

choices.label: Break past and run
choices.approach: Freedom first; honor later.

outcome.text: Dust and speed. Quill calls it strategic cowardice — fondly.

nodes.title: Quill's Lesson
nodes.body: “Steel answers collar-law better than ink,” Quill says, wiping ichor from the bolt like a clerk correcting a sum that insulted him. He drills stance, breath, and the difference between killing for a warrant and killing for rage — then takes a pull and adds, “Rage is fine. Just don’t let it hold the damn map. And don’t quit mid-swing.”

nodes.title: Campfire Question
nodes.body: Night fire snaps. Quill boils bitter tea that tastes like boiled warrants, spikes it from the flask, and asks what you will do if Lyra is already broken past rescue. The question sits between you like a third traveler with muddy boots and no manners. His scarred eye does not blink.

choices.label: I bring her home anyway
choices.approach: Refuse the premise that anyone is past saving.

outcome.text: Quill nods once. “Stubborn miracles. I hate miracles. I’ll still pack for them — and for you not quitting.”

choices.label: Then Candlemire pays in blood
choices.approach: If she is gone, Cade Mire still owns the debt.

outcome.text: Quill’s eyes glitter. “Justice with teeth. I can work with that — carefully, and drunk enough to be honest.”

choices.label: I will decide when I see her
choices.approach: Keep counsel until the yard is real.

outcome.text: “Wise or evasive — same coin until spent,” Quill murmurs. “Don’t spend it drunk. That’s my job.”

nodes.body: A farmstead burned for unpaid tribute; the killer rides with a brand that matches Cade’s outer seals like a signature on arson. Quill’s warrant names him Vern of Lowhedge. “Alive if possible,” Quill says. “Dead if the world insists on being itself.”

nodes.title: Vern Cornered
nodes.body: Vern sits against a tree with a leg broken by his own horse — comedy the forest did not request. He begs for Cade’s protection and offers a torn ledger page with women’s names. Lyra’s sits three lines down, sold as “song-thrall, unbroken.” Vern smiles like that should buy him sunrise.

choices.label: Bind him for trial
choices.approach: Alive for Quill’s warrant and a clean conscience.

outcome.text: Vern weeps gratitude he does not deserve. Quill stamps ALIVE like it hurts. The ledger page is yours.

choices.label: End him for Lowhedge
choices.approach: Justice for ash roofs; take the page either way.

outcome.text: The forest swallows the sound. Quill files the warrant CLOSED and does not congratulate you.

choices.label: Press for Candlemire routes
choices.approach: Pain or promise — get the eastern road correct.

success.text: Vern sketches fords and bribe posts. “Cade smiles when he sells people,” he spits. “You’ll hate how pretty it is.”

fail.text: Vern faints before finishing. You still have the ledger shred, a headache, and Quill’s unimpressed silence.

nodes.title: Which Warrant?
nodes.body: Three warrants hang like wet laundry: a goblin raid-chief, a barge cutter on the Brand-River feeder, and a knight who sold villagers under Cade’s quiet seal. Quill lets you choose the first debt to collect. “Pick the one that keeps you sleeping,” he says. “Or the one that doesn’t. Both teach.”

choices.label: Hunt the goblin chief
choices.approach: Clear hills so freefolk stop paying ‘protection.’

outcome.text: Quill packs snares. “Hills first. Rivers remember — and so do I.”

choices.label: Hunt the barge cutter
choices.approach: Follow water toward Candlemire’s quiet supply.

outcome.text: “Rivers teach patience,” Quill says, already smelling mud and worse math.

choices.label: Hunt the selling knight
choices.approach: Strike the soft armor of Cade’s respectables.

outcome.text: Quill’s smile thins. “Careful. Knights have friends with seals and soft hands.”

nodes.title: Reed Confession

fail.text: He spits mud and silence. You leave poorer in every sense.

nodes.title: Steward's Smile
nodes.body: A Candlemire steward offers “guest guidance” that is clearly a leash. He asks if you prefer the scenic cane walk or the efficient path past the collar yards. Quill’s eyes say both are traps; pick the useful one.

nodes.body: Bootleather remembers every mile better than maps ever will. A hanged poster of your face lies about the eyes — always the eyes. Another frame turns like a dusty page with teeth.

flagEchoes.line: Freemark law still tastes like silver you chose over easier blood.
Freemark’s rat still flinches in stories told two towns back.

nodes.title: Memory Shades
nodes.body: At a crossroads shrine, memory itself seems to ask whether you bury Candlemire’s name, museum it as warning, or rebuild over its footprint until children forget the smell of rings.

choices.label: Bury the name
choices.approach: Let the house become unmarked dirt.

outcome.text: Forgetting as mercy — debated, chosen.

choices.label: Keep it as warning
choices.approach: Tell the story until it cannot recruit.

outcome.text: Quill smiles like a teacher with better books.

choices.label: Rebuild over the footprint
choices.approach: Inhabit the wound until it is a kitchen.

outcome.text: Lyra likes kitchens better than monuments.

nodes.title: Free Horizon: Ash Aside
nodes.body: Crows argue over something dead you hope is not destiny. You catch yourself walking like cargo that learned a verb. Quill nods toward the darker horizon without ceremony.

flagEchoes.line: Quill’s displeasure rides quieter than anger and lasts longer.

nodes.title: Free Horizon: Quill's Aside
nodes.body: Dust counsel replaces court counsel without missing the bastard. Ash rolls a shoulder that still remembers iron’s manners. West is a rumor; east is a debt with a smile.

flagEchoes.line: Candlemire’s guest wing scratches your palm whenever you make a fist.

nodes.title: Free Horizon: Cane Wind
nodes.body: Quill hums a tune that never finishes the same way twice. Somewhere a horn asks a question steel usually answers. West is a rumor; east is a debt with a smile.

flagEchoes.line: Somewhere Cade’s riders still chase a river you invented — good.
Your near-fall oiled a latch — theater as lockpick, still working.

nodes.title: Free Horizon: Dust Mile
nodes.body: Quill refuses to advise and means it as the highest respect. Ash keeps vows that ledgers cannot translate into inventory. Quill tips a ruined hat at unfinished justice and means goodbye.

flagEchoes.line: Freemark’s rat still flinches in stories told two towns back.

nodes.title: Free Horizon: Quiet Counsel
nodes.body: Camp smoke writes temporary law across the pines. Freedom still feels like hunger wearing better boots. Night takes attendance; you answer as a freeman.

flagEchoes.line: Quiet passage bought with Quill’s settled debt still oils tonight’s miles.

nodes.title: Free Horizon: Road Hygiene
nodes.body: Remnant coin buys late knives and earlier, uglier panic. Ash keeps counsel; Quill keeps a ledger of silences and a flask of worse truths. Another frame turns like a dusty page with teeth.

flagEchoes.line: You refused Quill’s side warrant; Lyra-first still sits between you like weather.
Your hard hand has a reputation that arrives before your freemark does.

nodes.title: Remnant Coin
nodes.body: A last purse of Cade’s coin hires desperate blades on the western track. Mark-hunters who missed earlier chapters arrive late and louder. Dust settles the debate.

choices.label: Stand and fight
choices.approach: Use hotbar steel; clocks stay off — take your time.

outcome.text: Late Mark-Hunters falls. The road tastes like iron and relief.

choices.label: Outwit the terrain
choices.approach: Use cover, noise, and Quill’s nasty little tricks.

success.text: The ambush becomes your board. They break first.

fail.text: Clever turns loud. You bleed for the lesson and win anyway.

choices.label: Break past and run
choices.approach: Freedom first; honor later.

outcome.text: Dust and speed. Quill calls it strategic cowardice — fondly.

nodes.title: Free Horizon: Old Hunger
nodes.body: Steel rides light; the flask in Quill’s coat does not. Somewhere a horn asks a question steel usually answers. Quill tips his ruined hat at unfinished justice and takes a pull.

flagEchoes.line: Mud still remembers the night you chose noisy mercy over clean maps.

nodes.title: Free Horizon: Freemark Thoughts
nodes.body: Night insects keep score of every secret step you still take. Ash counts exits without moving his mouth — old habit, good habit. The Wilderland watches and keeps its receipts.

flagEchoes.line: A steward’s soft knock lives in your wrist like a second pulse.

nodes.title: Free Horizon: Ledger Weather
nodes.body: A child’s chalk freemark on a fence post has already been scrubbed. A road-vermin kid offers directions priced in half-truths. Quill tips a ruined hat at unfinished justice and means goodbye.

flagEchoes.line: Lyra’s sale lot number ticks behind your eyes like a fuse.
Posing as Cade’s muscle left a bruise on your pride and a useful swagger.

nodes.title: Free Horizon: Steel Practice
nodes.body: Distant cane fields clap like soft applause for bad systems. Tonight’s camp will argue theology with mosquitoes and memory. You walk west as inventory that refused the count and won.

flagEchoes.line: River cold still lives in your bones — you beat the Brand-River once.

nodes.title: Free Horizon: Night Watch
nodes.body: A dead mule teaches economics better than any clerk. Mercy, fire, and shared dawn wait like weather you finally choose. The next mile does not apologize.

flagEchoes.line: Gate smiles still taste like the purse you widened on the way in.

nodes.title: Free Horizon: Iron Humor
nodes.body: Horizon light writes nothing but possibility — terrifying, holy. Ash counts exits without moving his mouth — old habit, good habit. Somewhere east, a ledger page waits to be rewritten.

flagEchoes.line: Posing as Cade’s muscle left a bruise on your pride and a useful swagger.
You refused Quill’s categories — still a vow with a sword, not a category.

nodes.title: Free Horizon
nodes.body: The frame opens onto sky without chimneys writing orders on it. Ash and Lyra stand in weather that asks no brand. Quill waits without advising — binder to the end, friend on purpose.

flagEchoes.line: A gallery key warms against your ribs like a conspiracy with teeth.

nodes.title: Free Horizon: Collar Echo
nodes.body: A roadside shrine wears three names and one empty nail. The ending triad is not a trick — it is authorship with consequences. Quill tips his ruined hat at unfinished justice and takes a pull.

flagEchoes.line: False papers held. Courage still holds their corners when clerks stare.

nodes.title: Free Horizon: Warrant Weather
nodes.body: Dust counsel replaces court counsel without missing the bastard. You trade a story for water and keep the ending private. West is a rumor; east is a debt with a smile.

flagEchoes.line: The window tap — her name, your risk — still echoes under gallery silk.

nodes.title: Free Horizon: Hard Mercy
nodes.body: A hanged scarecrow wears Cade’s check like a joke that landed. Ash counts exits without moving his mouth — old habit, good habit. You walk on because stopping is how numbers return.

flagEchoes.line: Freemark proof upstairs means Lyra is not a ghost you invented.
Quiet passage bought with Quill’s settled debt still oils tonight’s miles.

nodes.title: Free Horizon: Vermin Bargain
nodes.body: Distant cane fields clap like soft applause for bad systems. Last enforcers make theology out of leftover steel and lose. Somewhere east, a ledger page waits to be rewritten.

flagEchoes.line: Midnight thralls still count on the pact you whispered in kitchen steam.

nodes.title: Horizon Vote
nodes.body: Candlemire’s smoke thins behind you like a bad religion losing believers. Quill waits without advising — the highest respect he knows. Lyra’s quiet is not emptiness; it is a question about what kind of free you will be, and whether free gets to invent new cages.

choices.label: Take the merciful road
choices.approach: Leave leftover justice to other hunters; walk west.

outcome.text: The Wilderland softens half a degree. Not forgiveness — room.

choices.label: Finish hard justice
choices.approach: Burn what remains of Cade’s legal skin.

outcome.text: Fire writes the last ledger. Names stop being inventory.

choices.label: Found a shared dawn
choices.approach: Stay and build a freemark hold that keeps no collars.

outcome.text: Hammers replace brands. Quill files a warrant against slavery itself.

nodes.title: The Merciful Road
nodes.body: West opens without a brand on any skyline. Lyra walks beside you; Quill tips his hat and files no warrant for kindness. The Wilderland keeps room for people who leave cages empty.

nodes.title: Hard Justice
nodes.body: Candlemire’s last seals blacken. Hard justice is a fire that warms freed hands and frightens soft thieves. You do not apologize for the smoke.

nodes.title: Shared Dawn
nodes.body: A freemark hold rises where cane once hid screams. Shared dawn means watches, kitchens, and laws that refuse inventory of people. Quill stays to teach binders a better trade.

endings.title: The Merciful Road
endings.blurb: Ash and Lyra leave Cade's fall to other hunters and walk west under open sky.

endings.title: Hard Justice
endings.blurb: Candlemire burns by Ash's hand; the Wilderland remembers the fire longer than the names.

endings.title: Shared Dawn
endings.blurb: Quill, Ash, and the freed march forge a freehold that keeps no brands and no guests with chains.

encounterCadence.note: Engine-side: roll next random ambush after 10–20 frames advance; prefer chapter enemyThemes.
